content: Authentication uses JWT tokens stored in httpOnly cookies
User data is persisted in PostgreSQL with the users table at the schema root
API rate limiting is implemented via Redis counter with sliding window
Error handling follows the Result type pattern: never throw, always return discriminated union
Configuration is loaded from config.yaml at the project root, never from environment variables
Tests run on Vitest, not Jest — Vitest is faster and has native TypeScript support
CI is configured via GitHub Actions in .github/workflows/ — no Travis, no CircleCI
Deployment uses Docker Compose on a single VPS — not Heroku, not Vercel, not Kubernetes
Logging uses pino for structured JSON output, not winston
Environment variables are managed with dotenv, never with env-cmd
The frontend is React 19 with TypeScript strict mode enabled
Styling uses Tailwind utility classes — no CSS modules, no styled-components
State management is Zustand for client state, React Query for server state
Form validation uses Zod schemas shared between client and server
Database migrations are managed by Drizzle ORM with versioned SQL files
API routes follow RESTful conventions with /api/v1 prefix
All API responses use snake_case for JSON keys, not camelCase
Background jobs are processed by BullMQ on Redis
Email is sent via Resend, not SendGrid
File uploads go to S3 with CloudFront in front, never to local disk
The monorepo uses pnpm workspaces, not npm workspaces or yarn
TypeScript builds with tsc for production, tsx for development
Linting is ESLint flat config with the typescript-eslint preset
Formatting is Prettier with no semicolons and 2-space indent
Pre-commit hooks run via Husky + lint-staged
API documentation is generated from OpenAPI specs in /api/openapi.yaml
The team uses Conventional Commits for commit messages
PRs require at least one approval before merge to main
Releases are cut with changesets and published by a release-please bot
Secrets are stored in 1Password CLI, never in .env files committed to git
All PRs run the full test suite before merge — no merge-on-green bypass
The database uses UUIDs for primary keys, never auto-incrementing integers
Time is always stored as UTC ISO 8601 strings, never as Unix timestamps
Money is stored as integer cents, never as floats
The codebase uses path aliases: @/components, @/lib, @/hooks
Server actions are preferred over REST endpoints for internal mutations
WebSockets are used for real-time features via the ws library, not Socket.IO
Search is powered by Meilisearch, not Elasticsearch or Algolia
The team follows trunk-based development with feature flags for incomplete work
Observability uses OpenTelemetry, with traces shipped to Honeycomb
Error tracking is Sentry on both client and server
Feature flags are managed by LaunchDarkly
The product analytics tool is PostHog, self-hosted
Customer support is handled by Intercom
Design assets live in Figma with the engineering team having read access
Code review feedback is addressed in the same PR, not in follow-up commits
The team holds a daily standup at 10:00 CET, async-first via Slack threads
Sprint planning is bi-weekly on Mondays, retros on Fridays
The staging environment mirrors production with synthetic data, not real customer data
Backups run hourly to S3 with 30-day retention, tested monthly
